module pe (
    input clk,
    input rst,
    input [31:0] a,
    input [31:0] b,
    output reg [31:0] c
);

always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
    
    if(rst) begin
        
        c <= 32'b0;
    end else begin
        
        c <= c + (a * b);
    end
end
endmodule

<module id="serial2parallel">
    <description>Serial-to-Parallel Conversion Circuit</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="din_serial">Serial input data.</input>
        <input id="din_valid">Validity signal for input data.</input>
        <output id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data (8 bits wide).</output>
        <output id="dout_valid">Validity signal for the output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="COUNTER_WIDTH">4</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="cnt" width_description="[COUNTER_WIDTH-1:0]">Counter to keep track of the number of serial input data bits received.</reg>
        <reg id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data register.</reg>
        <reg id="dout_valid">Valid output signal register.</reg>
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of clk
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                cnt <= 0;
                dout_parallel <= 0;
                dout_valid <= 0;
            end else begin
                if (din_valid) begin
                    dout_parallel[cnt] <= din_serial;
                    cnt <= cnt + 1;
                    
                    // Check if all 8 serial data bits have been received
                    if (cnt == 7) begin
                        dout_valid <= 1;
                        cnt <= 0; // Reset the counter after processing 8 bits
                    end else begin
                        dout_valid <= 0;
                    end
                end
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
</module>

<module id="multi_8bit">
    <description>Multiplies two 8-bit operands using shift-and-add method.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="A" width_description="[7:0]">First 8-bit input operand (multiplicand).</input>
        <input id="B" width_description="[7:0]">Second 8-bit input operand (multiplier).</input>
        <output id="product" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output representing the product of the two 8-bit inputs.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Initialize variables
        integer i;
        reg [15:0] temp_product = 0;
        reg [7:0] multiplicand = A;
        
        // Perform multiplication using shift-and-add method
        for (i = 0; i < 8; i = i + 1) begin
            if (B[i]) begin
                temp_product = temp_product + (multiplicand << i);
            end
        end
        
        // Assign final product to output port
        assign product = temp_product;
    </implementation>
</module>

    <description>data width convertion from 8 to 16</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">clock</input>
        <input id="rst_n">active low reset</input>
        <input id="valid_in">valid input</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data</input>
        <output id="valid_out">valid output</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output data</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // TODO: implement your solution here
        
        reg [15:0] data_out;
        reg [7:0] data_lock;
        reg flag;
        
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                data_out <= 0;
            end else begin
                if (flag && valid_in) begin
                    data_out <= {data_lock,data_in};
                end
            end
        end
        
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                flag <= 0;
            end else begin
                if (valid_in) begin
                    flag <= ~flag;
                end
            end
        end
        
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                data_lock <= 0;
            end else begin
                if (valid_in && !flag) begin
                    data_lock <= data_in;
                end
            end
        end
        
        assign valid_out = flag & valid_in;
        assign data_out = data_out;
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="multi_16bit">
    <description>Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Chip clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="start">Chip enable signal to initiate the multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="ain" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand (a) with a data width of 16 bits.</input>
        <input id="bin" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier (b) with a data width of 16 bits.</input>
        <output id="yout" width_description="[31:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 32 bits.</output>
        <output id="done">Chip output flag signal. Defined as 1 indicates the completion of the multiplication operation.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="IDLE">0</constant>
        <constant id="SHIFT_ACCUMULATE">1</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <signal id="i" type="reg" width_description="[4:0]">Shift count register initialized to 0.</signal>
        <signal id="done_r" type="reg">Multiplication completion flag initialized to 0.</signal>
        <signal id="areg" type="reg" width_description="[15:0]">Register to store the multiplicand (a).</signal>
        <signal id="breg" type="reg" width_description="[15:0]">Register to store the multiplier (b).</signal>
        <signal id="yout_r" type="reg" width_description="[31:0]">Register to store the product output (yout).</signal>
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Reset the shift count register to 0
                i <= 0;
            end else if (start && i < 17) begin
                // Increment the shift count register by 1
                i <= i + 1;
            end else begin
                // Reset the shift count register to 0
                i <= 0;
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Set the multiplication completion flag to 0
                done_r <= 0;
            end else if (i == 16) begin
                // Set the multiplication completion flag to 1
                done_r <= 1;
            end else if (i == 17) begin
                // Reset the multiplication completion flag to 0
                done_r <= 0;
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Initialize the multiplicand register to 0
                areg <= 0;
                // Initialize the multiplier register to 0
                breg <= 0;
                // Initialize the product output register to 0
                yout_r <= 0;
            end else if (start) begin
                // Perform shift and accumulate operation
                case(i)
                    0: begin
                        // Store the multiplicand (a) in the multiplicand register
                        areg <= ain;
                        // Store the multiplier (b) in the multiplier register
                        breg <= bin;
                    end
                    default: begin
                        // Accumulate the product output based on the current bit of the multiplicand register
                        if(areg[i-1]) begin
                            // Shift the multiplier register by i-1 positions and append zeros at the least significant bit positions
                            yout_r <= yout_r + {breg[15:0], {(i-1){1'b0}}};
                        end
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal. If the reset signal is low, initialize the shift count register to 0. Otherwise, increment the shift count register by 1 if the start signal is active and the shift count register is less than 17. Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal. If the reset signal is low, set the multiplication completion flag to 0. Otherwise, set the multiplication completion flag to 1 if the shift count register is equal to 16, and reset the multiplication completion flag to 0 if the shift count register is equal to 17. Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal. If the reset signal is low, initialize the multiplicand register to 0, the multiplier register to 0, and the product output register to 0. Otherwise, perform shift and accumulate operation based on the current bit of the multiplicand register. If the start signal is active, store the multiplicand (a) in the multiplicand register and the multiplier (b) in the multiplier register. For shift counts greater than 0 and less than 17, if the bit at position i-1 in the multiplicand register is high, accumulate the product output by shifting the multiplier register by i-1 positions and appending zeros at the least significant bit positions.</implementation>
    <task>
        <id>reset</id>
        <description>Reset the shift count register to 0.</description>
    </task>
    <task>
        <id>increment_shift_count</id>
        <description>Increment the shift count register by 1 if the start signal is active and the shift count register is less than 17.</description>
    </task>
    <task>
        <id>set_done_flag</id>
        <description>Set the multiplication completion flag to 1 if the shift count register is equal to 16, and reset the multiplication completion flag to 0 if the shift count register is equal to 17.</description>
    </task>
    <task>
        <id>shift_accumulate_operation</id>
        <description>Perform shift and accumulate operation based on the current bit of the multiplicand register. If the start signal is active, store the multiplicand (a) in the multiplicand register and the multiplier (b) in the multiplier register. For shift counts greater than 0 and less than 17, if the bit at position i-1 in the multiplicand register is high, accumulate the product output by shifting the multiplier register by i-1 positions and appending zeros at the least significant bit positions.</description>
    </task>
</module>

<module id="freq_div">
    <description>Frequency Divider for generating different clock frequencies based on input clock.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="CLK_in">Input clock signal.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal.</input>
        <output id="CLK_50">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 2.</output>
        <output id="CLK_10">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 10.</output>
        <output id="CLK_1">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 100.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Counter for CLK_50
        reg [0:0] cnt_50;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or posedge RST) begin
            if(RST) begin
                cnt_50 <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                cnt_50 <= ~cnt_50;
            end
        end
        
        // Counter for CLK_10
        reg [3:0] cnt_10;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or posedge RST) begin
            if(RST) begin
                cnt_10 <= 4'd0;
            end else if(cnt_10 == 4'd4) begin
                cnt_10 <= 4'd0;
            end else begin
                cnt_10 <= cnt_10 + 1;
            end
        end
        
        // Counter for CLK_1
        reg [6:0] cnt_100;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or posedge RST) begin
            if(RST) begin
                cnt_100 <= 7'd0;
            end else if(cnt_100 == 7'd49) begin
                cnt_100 <= 7'd0;
            end else begin
                cnt_100 <= cnt_100 + 1;
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Edge detector for detecting rising and falling edges of a 1-bit input signal 'a'.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of reset
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // On reset, initialize the previous value of 'a' to 0
                prev_a <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                // Update the previous value of 'a' with the current value of 'a'
                prev_a <= a;
            end
        end
        
        // Combinational logic to detect rising and falling edges
        assign rise = (prev_a == 1'b0 && a == 1'b1);
        assign down = (prev_a == 1'b1 && a == 1'b0);
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>A simplified radix-2 divider for 8-bit signed or unsigned integers.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">The clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst">The reset signal to initialize or reset the module.</input>
        <input id="sign">Indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">The 8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">The 8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">A 1-bit input signal indicating that a valid operation request is present.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">A 1-bit input signal indicating that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">A 1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">The 16-bit output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">Idle state.</state>
        <state id="DIVIDE">Divide state.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="res_valid_reg" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="start_cnt" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="cnt" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="neg_divisor" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="dividend_sign" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="divisor_sign" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="dividend_abs" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="divisor_abs" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <reg id="result_reg" type="DFF" init_value="0"/>
        <wire id="sub_result" width_description="[9:0]"/>
        <wire id="carry_out"/>
        <wire id="mux_out"/>
        <assign id="sub_result" value="{SR[8], SR} + {NEG_DIVISOR[8], NEG_DIVISOR}"/>
        <assign id="carry_out" value="~sub_result[9]"/>
        <assign id="mux_out" value={sub_result[8:0]} & {9{carry_out}} | {sub_result[8:0]} & ~{9{carry_out}});
        <always_comb>
            case(cnt)
                8'h0: result_reg = {SR[8:0], 1'b0};
                default: result_reg = {mux_out, SR[8:1]};
            endcase
        end
        <always @(posedge clk or posedge rst)> 
            if(rst) begin
                res_valid_reg <= 1'b0;
                start_cnt <= 1'b0;
                cnt <= 1'b0;
                neg_divisor <= 1'b0;
                dividend_sign <= 1'b0;
                divisor_sign <= 1'b0;
                dividend_abs <= 1'b0;
                divisor_abs <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                if(opn_valid && !res_valid_reg) begin
                    dividend_sign <= sign ? dividend[7] : 1'b0;
                    divisor_sign <= sign ? divisor[7] : 1'b0;
                    dividend_abs <= sign ? (dividend[7] ? (~dividend + 1'b1) : dividend) : dividend;
                    divisor_abs <= sign ? (divisor[7] ? (~divisor + 1'b1) : divisor) : divisor;
                    SR <= {dividend_abs, 1'b0};
                    NEG_DIVISOR <= ~(divisor_abs) + 1'b1;
                    cnt <= 1'b1;
                    start_cnt <= 1'b1;
                end else if(start_cnt) begin
                    if(cnt[7]) begin
                        cnt <= 1'b0;
                        start_cnt <= 1'b0;
                        SR <= result_reg;
                    end else begin
                        cnt <= cnt + 1'b1;
                        SR <= {mux_out, SR[8:1]};
                    end
                end
            end
        end
        <always @(posedge clk or posedge rst)) begin
            if(rst) begin
                res_valid_reg <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                if(cnt == 8) begin
                    res_valid_reg <= 1'b1;
                end else if(res_valid_reg && res_ready) begin
                    res_valid_reg <= 1'b0;
                end else begin
                    res_valid_reg <= res_valid_reg;
                end
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        When opn_valid is high and res_valid is low, the module saves the inputs dividend and divisor.
        Initializes the shift register SR with the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit.
        Sets NEG_DIVISOR to the negated absolute value of the divisor.
        Sets the counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process.
        Division Process(If start_cnt is high, the module performs the following steps):
        If the counter cnt reaches 8 (most significant bit of cnt is set), the division is complete:
        cnt and start_cnt are cleared.
        Updates the shift register SR with the final remainder and quotient.
        Otherwise, the counter cnt is incremented, and the shift register SR is updated based on the subtraction result:
        Computes the subtraction of NEG_DIVISOR.
        Uses a multiplexer to select the appropriate result based on the carry-out.
        Updates SR by shifting left and inserting the carry-out.
        Result Validity:
        res_valid is managed based on the reset signal, the counter, and whether the result has been consumed.
        res_valid should be update directly, without passing middle register value.
        res_valid is set to zero if rst is high.
        Otherwise, if cnt reaches 8, res_ready is set to 1.
        Otherwise, if res_valid and res_ready is all high, res_valid is set to 0.
        Otherwise, res_valid is set to res_valid.
    </implementation>
</module>

module calendar (
    input CLK,
    input RST,
    output reg [5:0] Hours,
    output reg [5:0] Mins,
    output reg [5:0] Secs
);

always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
    if (RST) begin
        Secs <= 0;
    end
    else if (Secs == 59) begin
        Secs <= 0;
    end
    else begin
        Secs <= Secs + 1;
    end
end

always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
    if (RST) begin
        Mins <= 0;
    end
    else if (Mins == 59 && Secs == 59) begin
        Mins <= 0;
    end
    else if (Secs == 59) begin
        Mins <= Mins + 1;
    end
    else begin
        Mins <= Mins;
    end
end

always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
    if (RST) begin
        Hours <= 0;
    end
    else if (Hours == 23 && Mins == 59 && Secs == 59) begin
        Hours <= 0;
    end
    else if (Mins == 59 && Secs == 59) begin
        Hours <= Hours + 1;
    end
    else begin
        Hours <= Hours;
    end
end

endmodule

<module id="signal_generator">
    <description>Triangle Wave Signal Generator</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <output id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">Generated waveform signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="0">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="1">Incrementing state.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="state" width_description="[1:0]">Register to store the current state.</reg>
        <reg id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">Register to store the generated waveform.</reg>
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on positive edge of clk or negative edge of rst_n
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Reset condition: set state to 0 and wave to 0
                state <= 0;
                wave <= 0;
            end else begin
                // State machine to generate triangle wave pattern
                case(state)
                    0: begin
                        // Increment wave value by 1
                        wave <= wave + 1;
                        
                        // Check if wave has reached maximum value (31)
                        if(wave == 31) begin
                            // Transition to next state (incrementing state)
                            state <= 1;
                        end
                    end
                    
                    1: begin
                        // Decrement wave value by 1
                        wave <= wave - 1;
                        
                        // Check if wave has reached minimum value (0)
                        if(wave == 0) begin
                            // Transition back to initial state
                            state <= 0;
                        end
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>Generates a triangular waveform by incrementing and decrementing the wave signal.</implementation>
</module>

<module id="fsm">
    <description>Detection circuit for a single-bit input IN using a Mealy FSM.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="IN">Input signal to be detected.</input>
        <input id="CLK">Clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal to initialize the FSM.</input>
        <output id="MATCH">Output signal indicating a match condition.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="S0">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="S1">State after detecting '1'.</state>
        <state id="S2">State after detecting '10'.</state>
        <state id="S3">State after detecting '100'.</state>
        <state id="S4">State after detecting '1001'.</state>
        <state id="S5">State after detecting '10011'.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="current_state" width_description="[3:0]">Register to hold current state.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" width_description="[3:0]">Register to hold next state.</reg>
        <always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST)>
            if (RST) begin
                current_state <= S0;
            end else begin
                current_state <= next_state;
            end
        </always>
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <assign id="MATCH">Output signal indicating a match condition.</assign>
        <always @(*) begin
            case(current_state)
                S0: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = S1;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end
                end
                S1: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = S2;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S1;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end
                end
                S2: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = S3;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S1;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end
                end
                S3: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = S4;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end
                end
                S4: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = S5;
                        MATCH = 1'b1;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end
                end
                S5: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = S2;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S1;
                        MATCH = 1'b0;
                    end
                end
                default: begin
                    next_state = S0;
                    MATCH = 1'b0;
                end
            endcase
        end
    </logic_description>
</module>

<module id="div_16bit">
    <description>Divide a 16-bit number by an 8-bit number.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="A" width_description="[15:0]">Dividend.</input>
        <input id="B" width_description="[7:0]">Divider.</input>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">Quotient.</output>
        <output id="odd" width_description="[15:0]">Remainder.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Register declarations for storing inputs
        reg [15:0] a_reg;
        reg [7:0] b_reg;
        
        // Combinational logic for division
        always @(A or B) begin
            a_reg = A;
            b_reg = B;
            
            // Initialize variables
            integer i;
            reg [15:0] quotient;
            reg [15:0] remainder;
            reg [15:0] temp_remainder;
            reg [15:0] temp_quotient;
            reg [15:0] shifted_divisor;
            reg [15:0] shifted_remainder;
            reg [15:0] final_result;
            reg [15:0] final_odd;
            
            // Perform division operation
            for (i = 15; i >= 0; i = i - 1) begin
                // Shift the remainder left by one bit
                temp_remainder[i+1] = remainder[i];
                
                // Subtract the divisor from the shifted remainder
                if (temp_remainder[i+1] >= b_reg) begin
                    temp_remainder[i+1] = temp_remainder[i+1] - b_reg;
                    temp_quotient[i] = 1;
                end else begin
                    temp_quotient[i] = 0;
                end
                
                // Update the remainder
                remainder = temp_remainder;
            end
            
            // Assign the final results to the outputs
            final_result = temp_quotient;
            final_odd = remainder;
            
            // Assign the outputs to the corresponding ports
            result = final_result;
            odd = final_odd;
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

module JC_counter (
    input                clk,
    input                rst_n,
    output reg [63:0]     Q
);

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (~rst_n) begin
        
        Q <= 64'b0;
    end else begin
        
        if (Q[0] == 1'b0) begin
            Q <= {1'b1, Q[63:1]};
        end else begin
            Q <= {1'b0, Q[63:1]};
        end
    end
end
endmodule

<module id="JC_counter">
    <description>64-bit Johnson Counter</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal to initialize the counter.</input>
        <output id="Q" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit register representing the current count value.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n). If the reset signal is active (rst_n == 0), the counter value (Q) is initialized to 0. Otherwise, based on the least significant bit (Q[0]), the counter either increments or decrements its value by shifting the bits to the right and appending a 1 or 0 at the most significant bit position.</implementation>
</module>

<module id="multi_pipe_4bit">
    <description>4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier with two levels of registers.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[3:0]">Multiplicand input signal.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[3:0]">Multiplier input signal.</input>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Product output signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="size">Number of bits for the multiplier and multiplicand.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <assign id="mul_a_ext" value="{size{1'b0}}, mul_a}"/>
        <assign id="mul_b_ext" value="{size{1'b0}}, mul_b}"/>
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <genvar i;
            for(i = 0; i < size; i=i+1) begin : gen_partial_products
                <assign id="partial_product[i]" value="(mul_b_ext[i]) ? (mul_a_ext << i) : 'd0"/> // Partial product generation based on multiplier bits
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="register[0][0]" width_description="[7:0]"/>
        <reg id="register[0][1]" width_description="[7:0]"/>
        <reg id="register[0][2]" width_description="[7:0]"/>
        <reg id="register[0][3]" width_description="[7:0]"/>
        
        <always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                register[0][0] <= 8'd0;
                register[0][1] <= 8'd0;
                register[0][2] <= 8'd0;
                register[0][3] <= 8'd0;
            end else begin
                register[0][0] <= partial_product[0];
                register[0][1] <= register[0][0] + partial_product[1]; // Add first two partial products
                register[0][2] <= register[0][1] + partial_product[2]; // Add next two partial products
                register[0][3] <= register[0][2] + partial_product[3]; // Final addition
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="mul_out_reg" width_description="[7:0]"/>
        
        <always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                mul_out_reg <= 8'd0;
            end else begin
                mul_out_reg <= register[0][3]; // Output the final result
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Extends inputs, generates partial products, adds partial products, stores results in registers, and outputs the final product.</implementation>
</module>

module pulse_detect #(
    parameter s0 = 2'b00,
    parameter s1 = 2'b01,
    parameter s2 = 2'b10,
    parameter s3 = 2'b11
) (
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input data_in,
    output reg data_out
);

localparam PULSE_DURATION = 3;

reg [1:0] state;

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        state <= s0;
        data_out <= 1'b0;
    end else begin
        case (state)
            s0: begin
                if (data_in) begin
                    state <= s1;
                end
            end
            s1: begin
                if (data_in) begin
                    state <= s2;
                end
            end
            s2: begin
                if (~data_in) begin
                    state <= s3;
                end
            end
            s3: begin
                if (PULSE_DURATION == 1) begin
                    data_out <= 1'b1;
                    state <= s0;
                end else begin
                    data_out <= 1'b0;
                    state <= s0;
                end
            end
        endcase
    end
end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Pulse Detection Module</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="INITIAL">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="STATE_1">State after detecting first rising edge.</state>
        <state id="STATE_2">State after detecting second falling edge.</state>
        <state id="PULSE_END">State indicating the end of a pulse.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="state" width_description="[2:0]">Register to store the current state.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" width_description="[2:0]">Register to store the next state.</reg>
        <always_comb>
            case(state)
                INITIAL: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = STATE_1;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = INITIAL;
                    end
                end
                STATE_1: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = STATE_2;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = STATE_1;
                    end
                end
                STATE_2: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = PULSE_END;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = INITIAL;
                    end
                end
                PULSE_END: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = INITIAL;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = PULSE_END;
                    end
                end
                default: next_state = INITIAL;
            endcase
        </always_comb>
        
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(~rst_n) begin
                state <= INITIAL;
            end else begin
                state <= next_state;
            end
        end
        
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(~rst_n) begin
                data_out <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                case(state)
                    INITIAL: data_out <= 1'b0;
                    STATE_1: data_out <= 1'b0;
                    STATE_2: data_out <= 1'b0;
                    PULSE_END: data_out <= 1'b1;
                    default: data_out <= 1'b0;
                endcase
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>Sequential logic for pulse detection based on the state machine.</implementation>
</module>

                    <id_ref id="q[7]"/>
                </left_side>
                <right_side>
                    <id_ref id="d"/>
                </right_side>

<module id="accu">
    <description>Accumulates 4 input data and outputs the result.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data for addition.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating readiness for new data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating when 4 input data accumulation is reached.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]">10-bit output data representing the accumulated sum.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of clk or falling edge of rst_n
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Reset condition: Set all registers to zero
                cnt <= 0;
                data_out_reg <= 0;
                valid_out_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                // Combinational logic based on valid_in and cnt
                case ({valid_in, cnt})
                    2'b00: begin
                        // When valid_in is low and cnt is 0, do nothing
                        cnt <= 0;
                        data_out_reg <= 0;
                        valid_out_reg <= 0;
                    end
                    2'b01: begin
                        // When valid_in is low and cnt is 1, increment cnt
                        cnt <= cnt + 1;
                        data_out_reg <= 0;
                        valid_out_reg <= 0;
                    end
                    2'b10: begin
                        // When valid_in is high and cnt is 0, accumulate data_in
                        cnt <= cnt + 1;
                        data_out_reg <= data_in;
                        valid_out_reg <= 0;
                    end
                    2'b11: begin
                        // When valid_in is high and cnt is 1, accumulate data_in
                        cnt <= cnt + 1;
                        data_out_reg <= data_in + data_out_reg;
                        valid_out_reg <= 0;
                    end
                    2'b12: begin
                        // When valid_in is high and cnt is 2, accumulate data_in
                        cnt <= cnt + 1;
                        data_out_reg <= data_in + data_out_reg;
                        valid_out_reg <= 0;
                    end
                    2'b13: begin
                        // When valid_in is high and cnt is 3, accumulate data_in
                        cnt <= cnt + 1;
                        data_out_reg <= data_in + data_out_reg;
                        valid_out_reg <= 1;
                    end
                    default: begin
                        // Default case: Reset cnt and valid_out_reg
                        cnt <= 0;
                        data_out_reg <= 0;
                        valid_out_reg <= 0;
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
        // Assigning the output ports to their respective registers
        assign valid_out = valid_out_reg;
        assign data_out = data_out_reg;
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="parallel2serial">
    <description>Parallel to serial conversion, where every four input bits are converted to a serial one bit output (from MSB to LSB).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="d" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit parallel data input.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output.</output>
        <output id="dout">Serial output representing the converted data.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        Assign the most significant bit of the parallel input to the serial output (dout).
        On each clock cycle, if the counter (cnt) is 3, indicating the last bit of the parallel input,
        update the data register (data) with the parallel input (d), reset the counter (cnt) to 0, and set the valid signal (valid) to 1.
        Otherwise, increment the counter (cnt) by 1, set the valid signal (valid) to 0, and shift the data register (data) one bit to the left, with the most significant bit shifted to the least significant bit.
        
        Counter Register: If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the register (cnt) is incremented by 1.
    </implementation>
</module>

module RAM #(
    parameter WIDTH=6,
    parameter DEPTH=8
) (
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input write_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]write_addr,
    input [WIDTH-1:0]write_data,
    input read_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]read_addr,
    output reg [WIDTH-1:0]read_data
);

reg [WIDTH-1:0] ram [DEPTH-1:0];

integer i;

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        for (i = 0; i < DEPTH; i = i + 1) begin
            ram[i] <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
        end
    end
    else if (write_en) begin
        ram[write_addr] <= write_data;
    end
end

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        read_data <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
    end
    else if (read_en) begin
        read_data <= ram[read_addr];
    end
end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="RAM">
    <description>A dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6, initialized to 000000. Supports simultaneous read and write operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="write_en">Write enable signal for initiating write operations.</input>
        <input id="write_addr" width_description="[5:0]">Address for write operations.</input>
        <input id="write_data" width_description="[5:0]">Data to be written to the RAM.</input>
        <input id="read_en">Read enable signal for initiating read operations.</input>
        <input id="read_addr" width_description="[5:0]">Address for read operations.</input>
        <output id="read_data" width_description="[5:0]">Output data read from the RAM.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="WIDTH">6</constant>
        <constant id="DEPTH">8</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <reg id="read_data" width_description="[5:0]">Register to hold the read data.</reg>
        <reg id="RAM" width_description="[DEPTH-1:0][WIDTH-1:0]">Array to store the RAM data.</reg>
        
        // Initialize RAM contents to 000000
        integer i, j;
        initial begin
            for(i = 0; i < DEPTH; i = i + 1) begin
                for(j = 0; j < WIDTH; j = j + 1) begin
                    RAM[i][j] = 1'b0;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic for writing data to RAM
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                for(i = 0; i < DEPTH; i = i + 1) begin
                    for(j = 0; j < WIDTH; j = j + 1) begin
                        RAM[i][j] <= 1'b0;
                    end
                end
            end else if(write_en) begin
                RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic for reading data from RAM
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                read_data <= 6'b000000;
            end else if(read_en) begin
                read_data <= RAM[read_addr];
            end else begin
                read_data <= 6'b000000;
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>Describes the dual-port RAM functionality, including initialization, writing, and reading operations.</implementation>
</module>

<module id="synchronizer">
    <description>A multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer. Data remains constant during the period when data_en is high, and data_en is high for at least 3 clk_b clock cycles. When the value of data_en is high, data can be synchronized. The data change frequency of data_in is very low. The change interval between two adjacent data is at least 10 clk_b clock cycles.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk_a">Clock signal A used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="clk_b">Clock signal B used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="arstn">Active-low reset signal A. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="brstn">Active-low reset signal B. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[3:0]">Input data signal of size 4 bits.</input>
        <input id="data_en">Input enable signal that controls the selection operation.</input>
        <output id="dataout" width_description="[3:0]">Output data signal of size 4 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Data Register
        reg [3:0] data_reg;
        always @(posedge clk_a or negedge arstn) begin
            if (~arstn) begin
                data_reg <= 4'd0;
            end else begin
                data_reg <= data_in;
            end
        end
        
        // Enable Data Register
        reg en_data_reg;
        always @(posedge clk_a or negedge arstn) begin
            if (~arstn) begin
                en_data_reg <= 1'd0;
            end else begin
                en_data_reg <= data_en;
            end
        end
        
        // Enable Control Registers
        reg en_clap_one;
        reg en_clap_two;
        
        always @(posedge clk_b or negedge brstn) begin
            if (~brstn) begin
                en_clap_one <= 1'd0;
                en_clap_two <= 1'd0;
            end else begin
                en_clap_one <= en_data_reg;
                en_clap_two <= en_clap_one;
            end
        end
        
        // Output Assignment
        reg [3:0] dataout;
        always @(posedge clk_b or negedge brstn) begin
            if (~brstn) begin
                dataout <= 4'd0;
            end else begin
                if (en_clap_two) begin
                    dataout <= data_reg;
                end else begin
                    dataout <= dataout;
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>Unsigned 8bit Multiplier using Pipelined Processing</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable signal that controls the multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 8 bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 8 bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 16 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">State IDLE = 0.</state>
        <state id="LOAD">State LOAD = 1.</state>
        <state id="CALCULATE">State CALCULATE = 2.</state>
        <state id="OUTPUT">State OUTPUT = 3.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="mul_en_out_reg" width_description="[3:0]">Register to hold the output enable signal.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_a_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplicand.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_b_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplier.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable to hold the first partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable to hold the second partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable to hold the third partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable to hold the fourth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[4]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable to hold the fifth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[5]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable to hold the sixth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[6]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable to hold the seventh partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[7]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable to hold the eighth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Sum variable to hold the result of adding the first two partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Sum variable to hold the result of adding the next two partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Sum variable to hold the result of adding the next two partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Sum variable to hold the result of adding the last two partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_out_reg" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the final product.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Register to hold the output enable signal
        reg [3:0] mul_en_out_reg;
        
        // Register to hold the multiplicand
        reg [7:0] mul_a_reg;
        
        // Register to hold the multiplier
        reg [7:0] mul_b_reg;
        
        // Temporary variables to hold the partial products
        reg [15:0] temp[0:7];
        
        // Sum variables to hold the results of adding the partial products
        reg [15:0] sum[0:3];
        
        // Final product register
        reg [15:0] mul_out_reg;
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Reset the output enable signal register
                mul_en_out_reg <= 4'd0;
                
                // Reset the multiplicand register
                mul_a_reg <= 8'd0;
                
                // Reset the multiplier register
                mul_b_reg <= 8'd0;
                
                // Reset the temporary variables
                temp[0] <= 16'd0;
                temp[1] <= 16'd0;
                temp[2] <= 16'd0;
                temp[3] <= 16'd0;
                temp[4] <= 16'd0;
                temp[5] <= 16'd0;
                temp[6] <= 16'd0;
                temp[7] <= 16'd0;
                
                // Reset the sum variables
                sum[0] <= 16'd0;
                sum[1] <= 16'd0;
                sum[2] <= 16'd0;
                sum[3] <= 16'd0;
                
                // Reset the final product register
                mul_out_reg <= 16'd0;
            end else begin
                // Update the output enable signal register with the input enable signal
                mul_en_out_reg <= {mul_en_out_reg[2:0], mul_en_in};
                
                // Update the multiplicand register with the input multiplicand
                if (mul_en_in == 1'b1) begin
                    mul_a_reg <= mul_a;
                end
                
                // Update the multiplier register with the input multiplier
                if (mul_en_in == 1'b1) begin
                    mul_b_reg <= mul_b;
                end
                
                // Generate the partial products based on the multiplier bits
                temp[0] <= mul_b_reg[0] ? {8'd0, mul_a_reg} : 16'd0;
                temp[1] <= mul_b_reg[1] ? {7'd0, mul_a_reg, 1'd0} : 16'd0;
                temp[2] <= mul_b_reg[2] ? {6'd0, mul_a_reg, 2'd0} : 16'd0;
                temp[3] <= mul_b_reg[3] ? {5'd0, mul_a_reg, 3'd0} : 16'd0;
                temp[4] <= mul_b_reg[4] ? {4'd0, mul_a_reg, 4'd0} : 16'd0;
                temp[5] <= mul_b_reg[5] ? {3'd0, mul_a_reg, 5'd0} : 16'd0;
                temp[6] <= mul_b_reg[6] ? {2'd0, mul_a_reg, 6'd0} : 16'd0;
                temp[7] <= mul_b_reg[7] ? {1'd0, mul_a_reg, 7'd0} : 16'd0;
                
                // Calculate the partial sums
                sum[0] <= temp[0] + temp[1];
                sum[1] <= temp[2] + temp[3];
                sum[2] <= temp[4] + temp[5];
                sum[3] <= temp[6] + temp[7];
                
                // Calculate the final product
                mul_out_reg <= sum[0] + sum[1] + sum[2] + sum[3];
            end
        end
        
        // Assign the output enable signal
        assign mul_en_out = mul_en_out_reg[3];
        
        // Assign the final product to the output port
        assign mul_out = mul_en_out ? mul_out_reg : 16'd0;
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>A simple traffic light controller with red, yellow, green, and pedestrian request inputs.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Request signal for pedestrians to cross.</input>
        <output id="clock" width_description="[7:0]">Internal counter value.</output>
        <output id="red">Red signal for the traffic light.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow signal for the traffic light.</output>
        <output id="green">Green signal for the traffic light.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="idle">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="s1_red">State 1: Red.</state>
        <state id="s2_yellow">State 2: Yellow.</state>
        <state id="s3_green">State 3: Green.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="cnt" width_description="[7:0]">Internal counter for timing control.</reg>
        <reg id="state" width_description="[1:0]">Current state of the traffic light controller.</reg>
        <reg id="p_red">Previous red signal.</reg>
        <reg id="p_yellow">Previous yellow signal.</reg>
        <reg id="p_green">Previous green signal.</reg>
        
        // State transition logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                state <= 2'd0;
                p_red <= 1'b0;
                p_yellow <= 1'b0;
                p_green <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                case(state)
                    2'd0: begin // idle
                        p_red <= 1'b0;
                        p_yellow <= 1'b0;
                        p_green <= 1'b0;
                        state <= 2'd1;
                    end
                    2'd1: begin // s1_red
                        p_red <= 1'b1;
                        p_yellow <= 1'b0;
                        p_green <= 1'b0;
                        if(cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= 2'd3;
                        end else begin
                            state <= 2'd1;
                        end
                    end
                    2'd2: begin // s2_yellow
                        p_red <= 1'b0;
                        p_yellow <= 1'b1;
                        p_green <= 1'b0;
                        if(cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= 2'd1;
                        end else begin
                            state <= 2'd2;
                        end
                    end
                    2'd3: begin // s3_green
                        p_red <= 1'b0;
                        p_yellow <= 1'b0;
                        p_green <= 1'b1;
                        if(cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= 2'd2;
                        end else begin
                            state <= 2'd3;
                        end
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
        
        // Counting logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                cnt <= 8'd10;
            end else begin
                if(pass_request && green) begin
                    cnt <= 8'd10;
                end else if(!green && p_green) begin
                    cnt <= 8'd60;
                end else if(!yellow && p_yellow) begin
                    cnt <= 8'd5;
                end else if(!red && p_red) begin
                    cnt <= 8'd10;
                end else begin
                    cnt <= cnt - 1;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Output assignment
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                red <= 1'b0;
                yellow <= 1'b0;
                green <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                red <= p_red;
                yellow <= p_yellow;
                green <= p_green;
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation></implementation>
</module>

    input 					rclk,

    output wire				wfull,
    output wire				rempty,
    output reg [WIDTH-1:0]	rdata
);

endmodule

module dual_port_RAM #(parameter DEPTH = 16,  parameter WIDTH = 8)
(
	 input wclk	,
	 input wenc	,
	 input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr  ,
	 input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata ,
	 input rclk	,
	 input renc	,
	 input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr ,
	 output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata 		
);

reg [WIDTH-1:0] RAM_MEM [0:DEPTH-1];

always @(posedge wclk) begin
	if(wenc)
		RAM_MEM[waddr] <= wdata;
end 

always @(posedge rclk) begin
	if(renc)
		rdata <= RAM_MEM[raddr];
end 

endmodule

samples.xml_description:
<module id="alu">
    <description>ALU for a 32-bit MIPS-ISA CPU.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="a" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="aluc" width_description="[5:0]">A 6-bit control signal for selecting the operation to be performed.</input>
        <output id="r" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit output representing the result of the operation.</output>
        <output id="zero">A 1-bit output indicating whether the result is zero.</output>
        <output id="carry">A 1-bit output indicating whether a carry occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="negative">A 1-bit output indicating whether the result is negative.</output>
        <output id="overflow">A 1-bit output indicating whether an overflow occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="flag">A 1-bit output representing a general flag, which is set based on specific operations (SLT and SLTU).</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="ADD">6'b100000</constant>
        <constant id="ADDU">6'b100001</constant>
        <constant id="SUB">6'b100010</constant>
        <constant id="SUBU">6'b100011</constant>
        <constant id="AND">6'b100100</constant>
        <constant id="OR">6'b100101</constant>
        <constant id="XOR">6'b100110</constant>
        <constant id="NOR">6'b100111</constant>
        <constant id="SLT">6'b101010</constant>
        <constant id="SLTU">6'b101011</constant>
        <constant id="SLL">6'b000000</constant>
        <constant id="SRL">6'b000010</constant>
        <constant id="SRA">6'b000011</constant>
        <constant id="SLLV">6'b000100</constant>
        <constant id="SRLV">6'b000110</constant>
        <constant id="SRAV">6'b000111</constant>
        <constant id="LUI">6'b001111</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <assign id="signed_a" width_description="[31:0]">Signed representation of input operand a.</assign>
        <assign id="signed_b" width_description="[31:0]">Signed representation of input operand b.</assign>
        <assign id="res" width_description="[31:0]">Register to store the result of the operation.</assign>
        <assign id="shift_amount" width_description="[4:0]">Shift amount for shift operations.</assign>
        <assign id="flag" width_description="[0:0]">Flag output for SLT and SLTU operations.</assign>
        
        <always_comb>
            <case id="operation_selection">
                <when condition="aluc == ADD">
                    res <= signed_a + signed_b;
                </when>
                <when condition="aluc == ADDU">
                    res <= a + b;
                </when>
                <when condition="aluc == SUB">
                    res <= signed_a - signed_b;
                </when>
                <when condition="aluc == SUBU">
                    res <= a - b;
                </when>
                <when condition="aluc == AND">
                    res <= a & b;
                </when>
                <when condition="aluc == OR">
                    res <= a | b;
                </when>
                <when condition="aluc == XOR">
                    res <= a ^ b;
                </when>
                <when condition="aluc == NOR">
                    res <= ~(a | b);
                </when>
                <when condition="aluc == SLT">
                    res <= {31'h00000000, signed_a < signed_b};
                    flag <= 1'b1;
                </when>
                <when condition="aluc == SLTU">
                    res <= {31'h00000000, a < b};
                    flag <= 1'b1;
                </when>
                <when condition="aluc == SLL">
                    shift_amount <= b[4:0];
                    res <= b << shift_amount;
                </when>
                <when condition="aluc == SRL">
                    shift_amount <= b[4:0];
                    res <= b >> shift_amount;
                </when>
                <when condition="aluc == SRA">
                    shift_amount <= b[4:0];
                    res <= $signed(b) >>> shift_amount;
                </when>
                <when condition="aluc == SLLV">
                    shift_amount <= a[4:0];
                    res <= b << shift_amount;
                </when>
                <when condition="aluc == SRLV">
                    shift_amount <= a[4:0];
                    res <= b >> shift_amount;
                </when>
                <when condition="aluc == SRAV">
                    shift_amount <= a[4:0];
                    res <= $signed(b) >>> shift_amount;
                </when>
                <when condition="aluc == LUI">
                    res <= {a[15:0], 16'h0000};
                </when>
                <default>
                    res <= 32'hzzzzzzzz;
                </default>
            </case>
        </always_comb>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>ALU performs arithmetic and logical operations based on the control signal (aluc) and updates the result (r) accordingly.</implementation>
</module>
